title: Role-Based Access Control — Ownable and AccessControl Audit
summary: Audit the access control architecture of a Solidity contract to verify that privileged functions are correctly gated, ownership transfer is two-step, admin roles are revoked post-initialization, and no role assignment bypasses timelock or multisig controls.
preconditions: Contract uses OpenZeppelin Ownable, Ownable2Step, AccessControl, or a custom access control modifier pattern.

cost_estimate.notes: Automated tools find privileged function candidates and single-step ownership patterns. Items 1c, 3, and 6 require business-logic understanding that cannot be automated.

checklist.item: Enumerate all privileged functions: those gated by onlyOwner, onlyRole, or any custom modifier that restricts callers. Produce a complete list with the contract name, function name, and modifier applied.
checklist.rationale: A complete privilege map is the prerequisite for all access control findings. Ungated functions that should be privileged are the most commonly missed finding in this category.

tooling.manual: Review all public and external functions; flag any that modify protocol state or configuration without a caller restriction.

checklist.item: For each privileged function in privileged_function_list, document the required role (e.g. MINTER_ROLE, DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, owner) and verify the modifier correctly enforces that role — no logic gaps or typos in role bytes.
checklist.rationale: Modifier names can shadow role identifiers or be applied inconsistently. A function labeled onlyAdmin may not actually check DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE if the modifier is custom and poorly implemented.

tooling.automated: slither --print function-summary — shows modifier list per function
tooling.manual: For each modifier, trace its implementation and verify the role it checks matches the intended role constant.

checklist.item: Flag any privileged function whose role restriction lacks a documented business justification. A function that can pause the protocol, upgrade the implementation, or drain funds must have a documented reason why that privilege exists and who holds it.
checklist.rationale: Privilege without documented intent is a governance risk, not just a security risk. Auditors and token holders cannot evaluate whether a privilege is appropriate if its purpose is not stated.

tooling.automated: none — requires business logic understanding and documentation review
tooling.manual: For each function in role_requirement_map, verify a documented justification exists in the audit report, NatSpec, or governance documentation.

checklist.item: Verify that ownership transfer uses a two-step pattern: transferOwnership() proposes the new owner, and acceptOwnership() must be called by the nominee to complete the transfer. A single-step transfer is a critical finding.
checklist.rationale: Single-step ownership transfer permanently bricks the contract if the new owner address is incorrect — a typo, wrong network, or compromised key. Two-step forces the nominee to prove they control the destination address.

tooling.automated: grep -rn 'Ownable2Step\|acceptOwnership\|pendingOwner' src/ — absence is a finding
tooling.manual: Verify transferOwnership() sets a pendingOwner rather than immediately overwriting _owner; verify acceptOwnership() is callable only by pendingOwner.

checklist.item: Confirm that renounceOwnership() cannot leave the contract in a permanently uncontrollable state. Either the function is overridden to revert, or all critical functions remain callable without an owner.
checklist.rationale: Renounced ownership is irrecoverable. If critical contract functions require owner-level access, renouncing permanently disables them. This must be an explicit, documented design decision — not an inherited function that was never considered.

tooling.manual: Trace all onlyOwner functions; determine if protocol remains operational with owner == address(0); document the finding either way.

checklist.item: Verify that DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (or equivalent highest-privilege role) is explicitly revoked from the deployer address after contract initialization completes. Check post-deployment state on testnet.
checklist.rationale: Contracts leaving the deployer as DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE in production create a single point of compromise: a stolen deployer key can instantly reassign all roles. This is the most commonly found critical access control bug in deployed contracts.

tooling.automated: cast call {contract} 'hasRole(bytes32,address)' $(cast keccak 'DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE') {deployer_addr} — should return false post-init
tooling.manual: Trace deployment script step-by-step; confirm the final step revokes DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE from msg.sender.

checklist.item: Verify that every role assignment and revocation is gated: no single EOA should execute grantRole() or revokeRole() in a single transaction without a timelock delay (minimum 48h) or multisig threshold (minimum 2-of-N).
checklist.rationale: Ungated role assignment means a compromised admin key can instantly escalate privileges, add malicious addresses to privileged roles, or disable security mechanisms. Timelocks and multisigs distribute the attack surface and give stakeholders a reaction window.

tooling.automated: slither --detect unprotected-upgrade (partial); check if grantRole routes through TimelockController
tooling.manual: For every grantRole/revokeRole call path: confirm it routes through TimelockController.schedule() with minDelay >= 172800 (48h) OR requires Gnosis Safe threshold >= 2.

checklist.item: Test role state under all deployment and upgrade paths: initial deploy, proxy upgrade, emergency pause and unpause, and ownership handoff to multisig. Confirm role assignments are correct in every end state.
checklist.rationale: Access control bugs appear most frequently at state transitions — upgrades and emergency procedures — not in the steady state. Each path must be explicitly simulated because role state is not always re-initialized correctly after an upgrade.

tooling.automated: forge test --match-test testUpgrade,testPause,testRoleHandoff (Foundry) — write explicit state-transition tests
tooling.manual: For each deployment and upgrade path: run the full script sequence in a fork environment; call hasRole() for all critical roles after each transition.

successConditions: privileged_function_list complete with zero ungated functions that modify state
role_requirement_map shows every modifier correctly enforces its intended role
unjustified_privilege_findings is empty or all findings have accepted documentation
Two-step ownership transfer confirmed in source and tested on testnet
renounceOwnership() overridden to revert or protocol documented as owner-independent
deployer holds DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE == false in post-initialization state
All grantRole/revokeRole paths route through timelock >= 48h or multisig threshold >= 2
Role state correct in all deployment and upgrade path end states
failureConditions: Any state-modifying function accessible without a caller restriction — CRITICAL
Modifier implementation does not match the intended role constant — HIGH
Single-step ownership transfer in use — CRITICAL
Deployer holds DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE in post-initialization production state — CRITICAL
Any grantRole/revokeRole path executable by a single EOA without timelock — CRITICAL
Role state incorrect in any deployment or upgrade path end state — HIGH

inputs.name: contract_source
inputs.description: Solidity source files (.sol) including proxy and upgrade logic

inputs.name: deployment_scripts
inputs.description: Deployment and initialization scripts to trace post-deploy role state

inputs.name: test_environment
inputs.description: Fork test environment for simulating deployment and upgrade paths

outputs.name: rbac_clearance
outputs.description: True when all 8 checks pass with zero critical or high violations

outputs.name: privileged_function_list
outputs.description: Complete inventory of privileged functions with role requirements and gating status

outputs.name: role_requirement_map
outputs.description: Map from function selector to role constant with modifier verification status
